channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: California's working class overlooked amid wildfires, US rep says
item.description: Rep. Maxine Waters, D-Calif., called for attention to the working class people in Altadena, California, who have “limited resources" amid the wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Prosecutors seek 15-year sentence for disgraced former NJ Senator Bob Menendez after bribery conviction
item.description: Prosecutors are seeking at 15-year sentence for disgraced former New Jersey Senator Bob Menendez, who was convicted in a bribery case after a nine-week-long trial.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Congress braces for California wildfire fallout
item.description: House lawmakers are bracing for what could be a contentious battle over emergency spending in the wake of the wildfires that are tearing through Southern California — an unprecedented disaster that’s already estimated to have caused more than $50 billion in damage in and around Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Many Democrats don't think they'll see a woman become president, AP-NORC poll finds
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Democrats are harboring strong feelings of stress and gloom as the new year begins. And many are questioning whether their party's commitment to diverse candidates — especially women — may lead to further political struggles in the Donald Trump era.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge who blocked release of Trump report was 'plainly' wrong, special counsel tells appeals court
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Justice Department has asked a federal appeals court to move swiftly in reversing a judge's order that had blocked the agency from releasing any part of special counsel Jack Smith's investigative report on President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Some US lawmakers want more Christianity in the classroom. Trump could embolden their plans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Conservative lawmakers across the U.S. are pushing to introduce more Christianity to public school classrooms, testing the separation of church and state by inserting Bible references into reading lessons and requiring teachers to post the Ten Commandments.
item.dc:creator: MORIAH BALINGIT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Biden is still considering pardons for people who have been criticized or threatened by Trump
item.description: President Joe Biden says he is still considering whether to give pardons to people who have been criticized or threatened by President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Zeke Miller And Colleen Long, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Supreme Court to weigh reinstating Obamacare care requirements struck down by lower court
item.description: The Supreme Court agreed Friday to consider some reinstating preventative care coverage requirements under the Affordable Care Act that were struck down by a lower court.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Whitehurst, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US streamlines deportation flights to China
item.description: The Biden administration repatriated more than 500 Chinese nationals in the last year, laying the groundwork for systematic deportations across the Pacific Ocean.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republicans blast 'joke' sentencing of Trump 10 days before swearing in
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump was sentenced for falsifying records just 10 days before he is set to take office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DHS extends TPS for Salvadorans, potentially complicating Trump's plans
item.description: The Biden administration on Friday extended a key humanitarian program for El Salvadoran nationals in a move that could complicate the incoming Trump administration’s plans to do away with long-term immigrant protections.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: President-elect Trump sentenced to unconditional discharge in hush money case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump was sentenced Friday in his hush money case, but the judge declined to impose any punishment.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Supreme Court seems likely to uphold Jan. 19 TikTok shut down
item.description: The case asks the high court to weigh the First Amendment against serious national security concerns.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats fume over Alito-Trump call
item.description: Senate Democrats are furious that President-elect Trump has reached out to conservative Justice Samuel Alito amid a push by Trump for the high court to halt his criminal sentencing.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden to deliver a capstone address on his foreign policy legacy as he makes way for Trump's return
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden plans to deliver a capstone address Monday on his foreign policy legacy, according to the White House.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jimmy Carter's funeral brings together 5 current and former US presidents to honor one of their own
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As they filed into the front pews at Washington National Cathedral, wearing dark suits and mostly solemn faces, five current and former presidents came together for Jimmy Carter's funeral.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court refuses Trump’s request to block Friday’s criminal sentencing
item.description: The Supreme Court in a 5-4 decision on Thursday refused President-elect Trump’s request to halt his criminal sentencing, now just hours away, closing off Trump’s last remaining pathway to avoid receiving his punishment for his 34-count felony conviction in New York.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Michelle Obama 'sends her thoughts and prayers' while absent from Carter's funeral
item.description: Michelle Obama wasn't among the mourners attending former President Carter's funeral service in Washington, but she says she's thinking of and praying for "everyone who loved and learned" from the late commander in chief.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump, Obama exchange pleasantries at Carter funeral after long-standing rivalry
item.description: President-elect Trump and former President Obama had an extended friendly exchange as they sat next to each other at the funeral services for former President Carter on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump blames Newsom for California's raging wildfires
item.description: The wildfires that started on Tuesday have killed two people and destroyed more than 1,000 structures.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Nicole Mitchell’s attorneys push back on effort to bar ‘senator’ mentions at burglary trial
item.description: Ahead of a Minnesota lawmaker’s trial on burglary charges, attorneys are going back and forth over whether she can be called “senator” in front of jurors.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: These Democrats flipped their votes on the Laken Riley Act
item.description: Seven House Democrats flipped their votes to "yes" on a GOP-led bill named after the slain Georgia student Laken Riley that would require detention of migrants arrested for theft.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Freedom Caucus holds its fire on Johnson’s strategy for passing Trump agenda
item.description: Members of the conservative House Freedom Caucus are holding their fire despite Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) bucking their preferred strategy for passing President-elect Trump’s agenda, arguing that the ultimate product is more important than the configuration.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Garland to release Smith's Jan. 6 report, hold back Mar-a-Lago report
item.description: Attorney General Merrick Garland plans to release only the volume of special counsel Jack Smith’s report dealing with Donald Trump’s plans to subvert the transfer of power after his loss in the 2020 election, holding back on sharing the Mar-a-Lago report while the president-elect's two co-defendants
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Colleges prepare for new legal and political terrain under Trump
item.description: Colleges and universities are preparing for new legal and political territory as President-elect Trump returns to office. Concerns for international students, GOP attacks on higher education and increased protests on campus are just some of the worries on administrators' minds.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Takeaways from Trump’s Mar-a-Lago press conference
item.description: In a press conference at his Mar-a-Lago club in Florida, Trump would not rule out using military force to seize control of the Panama Canal and Greenland, citing national security interests.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: What to know about Trump and his keen interest in Greenland
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has said multiple times that the U.S. should buy Greenland, an autonomous territory of Denmark. The sparsely populated island is geopolitically important and mineral-rich.
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster, MPR News

item.title: Wisconsin Republicans pushing to make voter ID a constitutional requirement
item.description: Other states have taken similar steps in recent years to put voter ID requirements in the constitution. Voters approved it in Mississippi in 2011 and North Carolina voters in 2018, while Minnesota voters rejected it in 2012.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota lawmakers prepare to take office
item.description: The Minnesota House will enter the 2025 legislative session with a new power structure and also a new home base down the block as its typical digs undergo construction.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Politicians reflect on January 6, shifting landscape of power transitions
item.description: The 2024 Presidential Election results have been officially certified by members of Congress. While the session typically happens without fanfare, there was extra security in place this time.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge holds Giuliani in contempt in Georgia election workers case
item.description: A federal judge held Rudy Giuliani in civil contempt on Monday for failing to comply with court orders in two former Georgia election workers’ efforts to collect their $146 million defamation judgment. At the end of a two-day hearing that saw Giuliani take the stand, U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5
